item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs rewrite for tone. Severe overuse of "appears to be" and similar phrases is pretty jarring in spots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda have mixed feelings about this. I like the previous and current versions of it. The only thing I would have liked to have been kept is the interview log between (I assume) SCP-990 and someone else since it made a more obvious connection between this article and SCP-990 if you've read SCP-990 before or are familiar with SCP-990 (or at least after I read the spoilers with the previous version). It feels a bit more like a DoA version of SCP-515 with some or all of the references to SCP-990 gone.

item.title: Re: Update
item.content:encoded: While I will miss the hall with the DoA anomalies, I think the update definitely improves the article by being less expository and a lot more focused. My upvote is strengthened.

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded: So, to address the criticisms issued here, I've gone and retooled the article and removed significant portions to make it fit. Hopefully, this improves it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
which is clearly Nobody talking to 990

Spoilers
That's not Nobody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, ARD summed up a lot of my thoughts on the piece as a whole up there. I don't know much about DoA, but this doesn't really convey those strong feelings that other DoA articles do. Overall, the article is fairly "meh".

I'd also like to comment on the use of the ARC designation, which honestly put a few more nails in this coffin for me. When I saw "SCP-5988-ARC" in chat, I mainly chose to read because I thought you were going to actually try and do something with ARC in-universe. Sadly, that didn't happen. You briefly touched on how the article was archived at the beginning, and then never expounded on it at all. It was honestly kind of a letdown, especially since I would have been extremely excited to see someone actually try and revive ARC and do something new with it. It basically left me feeling like I'd fallen for some clickbait that I was particularly excited for, only to find the less-than-stunning video underneath.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The very beginning, with the entity in the VR headset, and the very end, with the Telekill room, are the only parts from which I’m getting a real DOA vibe. Everything else is a shallow retread of 3790, and the video log — which is clearly Nobody talking to 990 — doesn’t do anything except point at the Foundation and note that they’re paranoid. It’s pointless exposition that squanders the article’s initial creep factor.

I absolutely love the vibe of Department of Abnormalities articles, just the blend of imagery, mystery, and vague implications that produces fundamentally simple yet unnerving stories, and you've perfectly executed that here. Plus this references 4099, which means I'm obligated to upvote it either way.

Can we get a Department of Abnormalities tag started??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, department of anomalies threequel. have my +1!

item.title: Damn YOU Popsioak for uploading your article sooner than me >:(
item.content:encoded:
Don't you open that Trapdooooor!
You're a fool if you dare!
Stay away from that Trapdooooor!
'Cause there's something down there!

For the record, I had this in my drafts for almost 3 years so you can't accuse me of riffing off of pop' s.

This was made to celebrate my first article hitting 100 upvotes. I made this way back then, but didn't really finish it until I decided that the best way to celebrate that milestone was to finish a draft that I've been sitting on for almost as long I've been on this site. Here's to many more!

spoilers
- close

Just like 3220 and 5238, this is an inversion of another popular Series 1 SCP. Specifically, SCP-990. This article contains references a couple S1 skips but only one of them is a merely a cameo to better help discover one of the identities of the subjects involved.

Thanks to Popsioak, Hexick, and
Tanhony for their crit.

Source links:
Hut
brain1
